exercises.name: Bench Press
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with feet firmly on the ground
Grip the barbell slightly wider than shoulder-width
Lower the barbell to your chest in a controlled manner
Press the barbell upward until arms are nearly fully extended
Repeat for desired number of reps
tips: Keep your shoulders retracted and chest up
Control the weight on the way down
Don't lock out completely at the top
Maintain a neutral spine throughout the movement

exercises.name: Incline Press
instructions: Set the bench to an incline of 30-45 degrees
Grip the dumbells at shoulder height
Press the dumbbells upward and slightly forward
Lower back to starting position with control
Repeat for desired number of reps
tips: The incline targets the upper chest more
Use controlled movements
Don't arch your back excessively
Squeeze your chest at the top

exercises.name: Dumbbell Flyes
instructions: Lie flat on a bench with dumbbells held above your chest
Arms should have a slight bend throughout
Lower the dumbbells in an arc motion
Go until you feel a stretch in your chest
Bring dumbbells back to starting position using the same arc
tips: This is an isolation exercise for the chest
Maintain a slight bend in your elbows
Control the weight throughout
Feel the stretch at the bottom of the movement

exercises.name: Push-ups
instructions: Start in a plank position with hands shoulder-width apart
Lower your body until your chest nearly touches the ground
Push yourself back up to starting position
Keep your body in a straight line throughout
Repeat for desired number of reps
tips: Bodyweight exercise - no equipment needed
Engage your core throughout
Don't let your hips sag
Modify by doing push-ups on your knees if needed

exercises.name: Machine Chest Press
instructions: Adjust the machine seat so handles are at chest height
Grip the handles firmly
Press forward until arms are nearly extended
Return to starting position with control
Repeat for desired number of reps
tips: Great for beginners - machine provides stability
Use full range of motion
Don't lock out your elbows
Sit with back firmly against the pad

exercises.name: Cable Crossovers
instructions: Stand in the middle of a cable machine
Set cables to shoulder height
Grab handles and step forward slightly
Pull cables together across your body
Return to starting position with control
tips: Great for chest isolation and pump
Squeeze your chest at the point of contraction
Control the weight on the way back
Use a slight forward lean for better contraction

exercises.name: Pull-ups
instructions: Grab the pull-up bar with hands shoulder-width apart
Hang with arms fully extended
Pull your body up until chin is above the bar
Lower yourself back down with control
Repeat for desired number of reps
tips: Engage your lats, not just your arms
Pull elbows down and back
Control the descent
Use assistance if needed

exercises.name: Lat Pulldowns
instructions: Sit at the lat pulldown machine
Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width
Pull the bar down to your chest
Return to starting position with control
Repeat for desired number of reps
tips: Great for beginners working toward pull-ups
Focus on pulling with your lats
Don't jerk the weight
Full range of motion is important

exercises.name: Barbell Rows
instructions: Bend at the hips until torso is nearly parallel to ground
Grab the barbell with a shoulder-width grip
Pull the barbell to your lower chest
Lower the bar back to starting position
Repeat for desired number of reps
tips: Keep your back straight throughout
Drive elbows back and up
Don't let the weight pull your shoulders forward
Use a controlled tempo

exercises.name: Dumbbell Rows
instructions: Place one knee on a bench
Hold a dumbbell in the opposite hand
Pull the dumbbell up to your hip
Lower back to starting position
Repeat for desired reps then switch sides
tips: Great for working unilateral strength
Keep your back flat
Pull elbow close to your body
Squeeze your lat at the top

exercises.name: T-Bar Rows
instructions: Straddle the T-bar machine
Bend at hips with chest up
Grip the handles and pull upward
Lower back to starting position
Repeat for desired number of reps
tips: Excellent for mid-back development
Keep your torso relatively stable
Focus on the squeeze
Don't use excessive weight

exercises.name: Reverse Flyes
instructions: Bend at the hips until torso is parallel to ground
Hold dumbbells with arms extended down
Raise dumbbells out to the sides
Lower back to starting position
Repeat for desired number of reps
tips: Great for posterior deltoids and upper back
Slight bend in elbows throughout
Control the weight
Squeeze shoulder blades together
